channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Sets Eight Key Financial Sector Priorities to Strengthen Inclusive and Sustainable Growth
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced eight critical areas in the financial sector aimed at driving sustainable economic growth and supporting national development goals. During the Second Financial ...

item.title: Tanzania MPs Call for New Approaches to Road Financing Amid Infrastructure Challenges
item.description: Members of the Tanzanian Parliament have raised concerns about the country's inadequate road infrastructure and funding issues, calling for reforms in how roads are financed. The MPs highlighted the p...

item.title: IRCC notifies thousands of asylum seekers of potential ineligibility under new immigration law
item.description: Canada's Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has begun sending letters to around 30,000 refugee claimants, warning that they may not qualify for asylum under recently enacted legislati...

item.title: Large group of Maoists surrender in Telangana, ending armed presence in the State
item.description: On April 10, 2026, forty-two cadres of the Communist Party of India (Maoist), including senior leaders of the People’s Liberation Guerrilla Army (PLGA) and the Telangana State Committee, surrendered t...

item.title: Efforts to uncover the truth of the October 29, 2025 elections intensify in Tanzania
item.description: Tanzania is witnessing growing efforts, both locally and internationally, to uncover the truth about the October 29, 2025 General Election. A presidential commission led by retired Chief Justice Moham...

item.title: Melbourne Homes Showcase: Historic Conversions and Modern Design
item.description: A new selection of Melbourne homes has been highlighted, each offering a unique blend of historical charm and contemporary living. The featured properties nod to the past while providing generous spac...

item.title: Former assistant of Gilgo Beach killer recounts her time working for Rex Heuermann
item.description: The article recounts the perspective of Donna Sturman, a former assistant to Rex Heuermann, the Long Island architect who pleaded guilty to eight murders tied to the infamous Gilgo Beach serial killin...

item.title: Katsina State Government Plan Big Mass Wedding To Support 1,000 Couples
item.description: The Katsina State Government don announce say dem go organize the first edition of mass wedding for about 1,000 couples wey dem select from all the 34 local government areas for the state. The plan na...

item.title: ADB forecasts steady economic recovery for Pakistan amid Middle East uncertainty
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has revised Pakistan’s economic growth forecast upward to 3.5 per cent for FY2026, citing signs of stabilisation and a rebound in key sectors such as manufacturing, in...

item.title: US and Iran Reach Ceasefire Following Pakistani-Mediated Talks
item.description: Following a tense standoff and a looming threat of military action, the United States and Iran have reached a ceasefire agreement brokered through discussions involving Pakistani Prime Minister Shehba...

item.title: Artist Transforms Discarded Brushes into Miniature Nature Scenes
item.description: Simon Brown is an artist with a unique and captivating approach to repurposing everyday objects. He rescues discarded brushes, weathered shoes, and other forgotten items, giving them new life and mean...

item.title: India Tests 1,000-Kilometre Quantum Communication Network with Indigenous Technology
item.description: India has successfully demonstrated a quantum communication network spanning 1,000 kilometres, entirely developed using homegrown technology. This achievement marks a significant milestone in the coun...

item.title: Canada Faces Challenges in Meeting Biodiversity Goals Amidst Private Funding and Natural Capital Accounting
item.description: Canada is grappling with the ambitious task of protecting its biodiversity and combating climate change by 2030. A new strategy, spearheaded by former Bank of Canada Governor Stephen Poloz, focuses on...

item.title: Concerns Over Expanding CSIS’s Role in International Espionage
item.description: A recent internal memo from the Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) highlights growing concerns about the agency’s evolving role in international espionage. The memo examines the potential i...

item.title: Layi Wasabi: From Law Graduate to Pan-African Comedy Star
item.description: Layi Wasabi, originally trained as a lawyer in Nigeria, has emerged as a prominent comedic content creator across Africa. He first explored performance arts in secondary school and continued acting wh...

item.title: Zanzibar Sees Significant Growth in Exports Driven by Tourism and Cloves
item.description: Zanzibar's economy is showing solid performance, as the latest Bank of Tanzania report reveals a sharp rise in exports of both goods and services for the year ending February 2026. Total exports reach...

item.title: Tanzania’s $3 billion investment in gas-powered truck assembly
item.description: Tanzania is set to assemble its first heavy-duty lorry powered by compressed natural gas by 2026, following a Sh7.8 trillion ($3 billion) investment in a new industrial complex in Bagamoyo. The projec...

item.title: Volkswagen ends US production of ID.4 due to falling sales and shifting market priorities
item.description: Volkswagen announced that it will halt production of its ID.4 electric SUV at the Chattanooga, Tennessee plant this April, marking a significant shift in the company's US manufacturing strategy. The d...

item.title: Why dengue is hard to control with vaccines, despite millions of infections every year
item.description: Dengue is a mosquito‑borne viral disease that every year affects millions of people in Asia, Africa and Latin America, and it is now spreading to new areas because of climate change and fast urbanisat...

item.title: A family discovers a massive 70,000-bee hive hidden inside their home walls
item.description: In a striking real-life story shared in The Guardian, a family experienced the astonishing invasion of more than 70,000 bees that had built an enormous hive within the walls of their house. It all beg...
